what_it_does: This file is a JSON allocation batch that lists six markdown documents in the claude-ds project, each with its relative path and absolute filesystem root, likely as input for a staged interpretation or build-prep pass.
observations: All entries share the same project ('claude-ds') and root ('/home/tim/company/design/claude-ds').
Every listed path sits under the 'analysis/' directory.
The set mixes fundraising artifacts ('capital-raise.md', 'deck1-2026.md') with design-process artifacts ('DIAGRAMS.md', 'FINDINGS-LOG.md', 'GUIDE.md', 'HANDOFF.md').
The file is a flat JSON array of uniform objects with 'project', 'path', and 'root' keys.
No code symbols are associated with this batch.
File naming uses a mix of kebab-case and all-caps for meta documents.
standouts: Groups both capital-raise/deck content and design-process documentation in one batch.
Uses an absolute, user-specific filesystem root.
Path 'wave-001/alloc/batch-003.json' signals a staged, numbered allocation scheme.
conventions: Follows a simple JSON array-of-records schema.
Uses absolute root path plus relative document path.
Consistent key set across all entries.
Naming uses kebab-case and uppercase for process-oriented files.
concerns: Absolute root '/home/tim/company/design/claude-ds' hardcodes a user-specific path.
No stated rationale for why these six files are grouped together.
No version, timestamp, or ordering metadata.
No symbols, so downstream consumers must treat these as document rather than code substrates.
notes: A wave-001 allocation batch that registers six claude-ds analysis markdown files for downstream interpretation.
questions: What criteria determine membership in batch-003?
Does 'wave-001' imply a sequence, and are batches ordered?

purpose_vs_actual: The file appears to be an allocation manifest, and its content matches that purpose: it lists project files with their roots.
intention_signals: It seems to be trying to define a scoped, project-local set of analysis and fundraising documents to be processed together as part of a build-prep or interpretive pipeline.
novelty: It treats markdown design and fundraising documents as allocatable units in the same batch, rather than separating code substrates from prose artifacts.
contribution: Provides a concrete, file-level inventory of claude-ds analysis documents with their filesystem roots, enabling a fused system to know which source documents belong to a given processing batch without relying on directory inference.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON allocation batch listing six markdown analysis documents under /home/tim/company/design/claude-ds for the claude-ds project, grouped as wave-001 batch-003 with no associated code symbols.
